“What a misfortune! Good Lord, what a misfortune!”

Raskolnikov pushed his way in as far as he could, and succeeded at last in seeing the object of the commotion and interest. On the ground a man who had been ru…

…outed, pushing to the front. “It’s a government clerk retired from the service, Marmeladov. He lives close by in Kozel’s house.... Make haste for a doctor! I will pay, see?” He pulled money out of his pocket and showed it to the policeman.…

…t. Turn round! I’ll pay, I’ll make it worth your while,” he muttered.

Katerina Ivanovna had just begun, as she always did at every free moment, walking to and fro in her little room from window to stove and back again, with her a…

…too, stood in hesitation.

“Polenka,” cried Katerina Ivanovna, “run to Sonia, make haste. If you don’t find her at home, leave word that her father has been run over and that she is to come here at once... when she comes in. Run, …

…, but Amalia Ludwigovna, and as I am not one of your despicable flatterers like Mr. Lebeziatnikov, who’s laughing behind the door at this moment (a laugh and a cry of ‘they are at it again’ was in fact audible at the door) so I shall alway…

…y through the crowd to the stairs. But in the crowd he suddenly jostled against Nikodim Fomitch, who had heard of the accident and had come to give instructions in person. They had not met since the scene at the police station, but Nikodim…

…s? And let them... that’s the way to learn not to!... Wait a minute, I’ll fetch Zossimov.”

Zossimov pounced upon Raskolnikov almost greedily; he showed a special interest in him; soon his face brightened.

“You must go t…

…By the way, Potchinkov’s house is only a few steps away. I certainly must go to Razumihin even if it were not close by... let him win his bet! Let us give him some satisfaction, too--no matter! Strength, strength is what one wants, you can…

…ho, though there was much she did not understand, understood very well that her mother needed her, and so always watched her with her big clever eyes and strove her utmost to appear to understand. This time Polenka was undressing her littl…

…nd toes turned out.

He was listening to what his mother was saying to his sister, sitting perfectly still with pouting lips and wide-open eyes, just as all good little boys have to sit when they are undressed to go to bed. A litt…

…ee from below that there was a light in Raskolnikov’s garret.

“Queer! Nastasya, perhaps,” observed Razumihin.

“She is never in my room at this time and she must be in bed long ago, but... I don’t care! Good-bye!”

…

…at’s brought him to this conclusion about you was your conversation to-day with Zametov.”

“Zametov told you all about it?”

“Yes, and he did well. Now I understand what it all means and so does Zametov.... Well, the fact…

…ut a hint that you know of it; I’ve noticed he is a ticklish subject; it was at Luise Ivanovna’s. But to-day, to-day it’s all cleared up. That Ilya Petrovitch is at the bottom of it! He took advantage of your fainting at the police station…

…a doctor! I will pay, see?” He pulled money out of his pocket and showed it to the policeman. He was in violent agitation.

The police were glad that they had found out who the man was. Raskolnikov gave his own name and address, a…

…a time, trying to drive out the people who came in from the stairs. Almost all Madame Lippevechsel’s lodgers had streamed in from the inner rooms of the flat; at first they were squeezed together in the doorway, but afterwards they overflo…

…! We knew them long ago. I say my prayers to myself as I am a big girl now, but Kolya and Lida say them aloud with mother. First they repeat the ‘Ave Maria’ and then another prayer: ‘Lord, forgive and bless sister Sonia,’ and then another,…

…

At that moment other steps were heard; the crowd in the passage parted, and the priest, a little, grey old man, appeared in the doorway bearing the sacrament. A policeman had gone for him at the time of the accident. The doctor changed…

…as you can.... He is injured, but not killed, believe me.... We shall see what the doctor says!”

Katerina Ivanovna ran to the window; there, on a broken chair in the corner, a large earthenware basin full of water had been stood,…

…f the road with a pair of spirited grey horses; there was no one in it, and the coachman had got off his box and stood by; the horses were being held by the bridle.... A mass of people had gathered round, the police standing in front. One …

…e horses were being held by the bridle.... A mass of people had gathered round, the police standing in front. One of them held a lighted lantern which he was turning on something lying close to the wheels. Everyone was talking, shouting, e…

…worse. That’s how it happened!”

“That’s just how it was,” a voice in the crowd confirmed.

“He shouted, that’s true, he shouted three times,” another voice declared.

“Three times it was, we all heard it,” shout…

…ticulated with effort.) “Prince Schegolskoy, a kammerjunker, had just come from Petersburg then... he danced the mazurka with me and wanted to make me an offer next day; but I thanked him in flattering expressions and told him that my hear…

…two samovars, bottles, plates and dishes of pie and savouries, brought up from the landlady’s kitchen. Raskolnikov sent in for Razumihin. He ran out delighted. At the first glance it was apparent that he had had a great deal to drink and, …

…ef, looked for her mother, went up to her and said, “She’s coming, I met her in the street.” Her mother made her kneel beside her.

Timidly and noiselessly a young girl made her way through the crowd, and strange was her appearanc…

…m of the staircase and stopped short a step above him. A dim light came in from the yard. Raskolnikov could distinguish the child’s thin but pretty little face, looking at him with a bright childish smile. She had run after him with a mess…

…elings at times, brother. But the lesson, the lesson you gave him to-day in the Palais de Cristal, that was too good for anything! You frightened him at first, you know, he nearly went into convulsions! You almost convinced him again of th…

…s past ten when he came out into the street. In five minutes he was standing on the bridge at the spot where the woman had jumped in.

“Enough,” he pronounced resolutely and triumphantly. “I’ve done with fancies, imaginary terrors…

scenes.locationDescription: On the street beside an elegant carriage
scenes.imageDescription: A bustling nighttime city street in 19th-century St. Petersburg under flickering gas lanterns. Crowds of anxious onlookers, including the police and a coachman wringing his hands, gather around the wheel of a fine carriage. Policemen with lanterns illuminate a bloodied, unconscious man on the cobblestones. Faces are tense and pale; people jostle for a look, attired in worn coats and shawls, with an air of shock and morbid curiosity.
scenes.imageSubtitle: A crowd gathers as Raskolnikov witnesses an accident beside an elegant carriage.

scenes.locationDescription: Marmeladov's cramped family room in Kozel's house
scenes.imageDescription: A dim, shabby room with peeling wallpaper and sparse furniture in a St. Petersburg tenement, lit by a faint single candle and some lantern light from the hall. Katerina Ivanovna, gaunt and flushed, paces the room wrapped in a thin shawl as Polenka undresses a sickly little boy on a chair, while a younger girl waits in rags by a folding screen. The chaotic atmosphere is heavy with exhaustion and poverty.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Polenka gently cares for her ailing brother as Katerina paces the room.

scenes.locationDescription: Inside Marmeladov's family room as injured Marmeladov is brought in
scenes.imageDescription: The previously quiet, cramped room suddenly fills with people as men carry in the bloodied, unconscious Marmeladov and lay him on a threadbare sofa. Katerina Ivanovna gasps in white-faced horror while her children huddle in fear. Raskolnikov, breathless and urgent, directs helpers, as a policeman stands to the side. The air is tense and frantic, lit harshly by lanterns and candlelight.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov arrives as Marmeladov is carried into his family's crowded room.

scenes.locationDescription: Marmeladov's family room, crowd gathering
scenes.imageDescription: A packed, stifling tenement room as curious neighbors crowd the doorway and press inside, smoke drifting in from the hall. Katerina Ivanovna angrily scolds the gawkers and stands protectively by Marmeladov. Madame Lippevechsel, a stern, formidable landlady, confronts her in the doorway; Mr. Lebeziatnikov peeks in, amused. The desperate, chaotic energy contrasts with the dim, uneven candlelight and flickers of lanterns from the corridor.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Katerina Ivanovna confronts the crowd as the room fills with curious onlookers.

scenes.locationDescription: Marmeladov's family room as the doctor arrives
scenes.imageDescription: The dim room grows tense as a precise, elderly German doctor in dark frock coat arrives and examines Marmeladov, aided by Katerina Ivanovna. The policeman stands by quietly as the candle flickers over the bloody, dying man. The atmosphere is thick with fear and finality; weary faces of the family, Raskolnikov, and the silent children are bathed in the uneven glow.
scenes.imageSubtitle: The doctor examines Marmeladov as Raskolnikov and Katerina Ivanovna look on.

scenes.locationDescription: Marmeladov's family room, final moments with priest and arrival of Sonia
scenes.imageDescription: A sorrowful, candle-lit room as the frail priest in vestments gives last rites to dying Marmeladov surrounded by his family on their knees in prayer. Katerina Ivanovna, hunched and exhausted, holds her children close. Sonia, pale and trembling in gaudy, tattered finery and feathered hat, stands hesitantly in the doorway. Faces in the crowd are illuminated by the single candle and the glimmer from the hall. The mood is one of solemnity, grief, and desperate prayer.
scenes.imageSubtitle: The priest arrives for Marmeladov's last rites as Sonia enters the crowded room.

scenes.locationDescription: Beginning of the street outside Kozel's house
scenes.imageDescription: A shadowy stairwell with gaslight pooling in the corners as Raskolnikov pushes out from the press of mourners at Marmeladov's door. Nikodim Fomitch, in uniform, encounters him in the stairwell as a priest descends behind. The fatigue and grief are etched in Raskolnikov's pale, blood-spattered face. The atmosphere is heavy with weary relief.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Polenka chases after Raskolnikov as he leaves Kozel's house at night.

scenes.locationDescription: At the bottom of the stairwell near Kozel's house
scenes.imageDescription: The dim base of a cramped stone stairwell, lit by a faint street lantern through a grungy window. Raskolnikov, pale and unsteady, pauses as Polenka—thin, barefoot, and earnest—runs up to him smiling, passing on Sonia’s message. The atmosphere is tender and intimate, tinged with childish sincerity and gratitude.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov meets a smiling girl with a message on the stairwell.

scenes.locationDescription: On the bridge over the river in St. Petersburg
scenes.imageDescription: A moonlit, deserted St. Petersburg bridge arches over inky water under a lamplit sky. Raskolnikov stands motionless, staring down contemplatively at the river, the city night stretching behind him. His silhouette is gaunt and solitary, framed by the empty expanse and chill air, embodying newfound resolution.
scenes.imageSubtitle: A crowd gathers as Raskolnikov witnesses a carriage accident on the bridge.

scenes.locationDescription: In the entry outside Razumihin's lodgings at Potchinkov's
scenes.imageDescription: At a noisy landing outside a large, brightly-lit apartment, Raskolnikov stands among serving maids bustling past with samovars and trays. The door to Razumihin’s lively room is wide open, revealing many guests inside. Razumihin, flushed and cheerful, emerges to greet Raskolnikov, who stands pale, disheveled, and weary amidst the hubbub.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov pauses in the entry as Razumihin greets him amid a lively gathering.

scenes.locationDescription: Streets of St. Petersburg, walking from Potchinkov's to Raskolnikov's
scenes.imageDescription: A quiet nighttime street glistening with recent rain, illuminated by sparse gaslights. Raskolnikov staggers, supported by a talkative Razumihin; both are wrapped in dark coats. Their faces are intent, Razumihin animated with drunken cheer, Raskolnikov pale and exhausted. The empty, silent city stretches behind them.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin reassures Raskolnikov as they walk through the nighttime streets.

scenes.locationDescription: Stairwell below Raskolnikov's garret
scenes.imageDescription: A shadow-filled stairwell inside a decaying Petersburg boarding house. At the foot of the narrow stairs, Razumihin and Raskolnikov pause, both gazing upward at a line of warm light glowing beneath the garret door above. The setting is drab and claustrophobic, with anticipation in the air.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumihin discover unexpected visitors in the garret.

scenes.locationDescription: Inside Raskolnikov's garret room
scenes.imageDescription: A small, bleak attic room under the eaves, lit by a solitary oil lamp. Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Dounia, elegantly dressed but weary, sit anxiously on the meager sofa, Nastasya stands by, while Raskolnikov and Razumihin enter. The women leap up and embrace Raskolnikov, who collapses, pale and overcome, in their arms. The atmosphere is charged with relief and shock, tears glistening in the gentle lamplight.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov faints upon reuniting with his mother and sister in his room.

entities.excerpt: Raskolnikov got up, and sat down on the sofa. He waved his hand weakly to Razumihin to cut short the flow of warm and incoherent consolations he was addressing to his moth

Raskolnikov got up, and sat down on the sofa. He waved his hand weakly to Razumihin to cut short the flow of warm and incoherent consolations he was addressing to his mother and sister, took them both by the hand and for a minute or tw…

…to cut short the flow of warm and incoherent consolations he was addressing to his mother and sister, took them both by the hand and for a minute or two gazed from one to the other without speaking. His mother was alarmed by his expression…

…stand it!”

“Come, mamma, come out of the room at least for a minute,” Dounia whispered in dismay; “we are distressing him, that’s evident.”

“Mayn’t I look at him after three years?” wept Pulcheria Alexandrovna.

…r, waiting for what would come next. Both of them had heard of the quarrel from Nastasya, so far as she had succeeded in understanding and reporting it, and were in painful perplexity and suspense.

“Dounia,” Raskolnikov continued…

…’ll run home in a twinkling--I’ve a lot of friends there, all drunk--I’ll fetch Zossimov--that’s the doctor who is looking after him, he is there, too, but he is not drunk; he is not drunk, he is never drunk! I’ll drag him to Rodya, and th…

…that’s what makes me... swear; don’t mind it....”

“But I’ll go to the landlady here,” Pulcheria Alexandrovna insisted, “I’ll beseech her to find some corner for Dounia and me for the night. I can’t leave him like that, I cannot!”…

…em all sorts of names just now, I do respect them all... though I don’t respect Zametov, I like him, for he is a puppy, and that bullock Zossimov, because he is an honest man and knows his work. But enough, it’s all said and forgiven. Is i…

…ith him now, and I’ll conduct you both home, you can’t be in the streets alone; Petersburg is an awful place in that way.... But no matter! Then I’ll run straight back here and a quarter of an hour later, on my word of honour, I’ll bring y…

…the landlady’s flat here. (Hard work I’ve had to persuade her!) And I’ll be in the kitchen. So here’s a chance for you to get to know her better.... It’s not as you think! There’s not a trace of anything of the sort, brother...!”

…

…astasya will stay with him now, and I’ll conduct you both home, you can’t be in the streets alone; Petersburg is an awful place in that way.... But no matter! Then I’ll run straight back here and a quarter of an hour later, on my word of h…

…spense.

“Dounia,” Raskolnikov continued with an effort, “I don’t want that marriage, so at the first opportunity to-morrow you must refuse Luzhin, so that we may never hear his name again.”

“Good Heavens!” cried Pulcher…

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's small room
scenes.imageDescription: A dimly lit, modest St. Petersburg lodging room in the evening, with threadbare wallpaper and sparse furnishings. Raskolnikov, gaunt and haunted, sits weakly on a worn sofa. Razumihin stands nearby, animated, with an earnest expression. Pulcheria Alexandrovna, middle-aged and anxious, sits on a chair near Raskolnikov, while Avdotya Romanovna, striking and proud, stands close, her hand trembling in her brother’s. All wear conservative, period-appropriate Russian attire; the room feels tense and sorrowful.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov struggles to speak with his worried mother and sister in his room.

scenes.locationDescription: Landing and stairway outside Raskolnikov's room
scenes.imageDescription: A narrow, shadowy stairway in a worn apartment building, gaslight flickering along cracked plaster walls. Razumihin, flushed and exuberant, passionately argues and gestures while holding both Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Avdotya Romanovna by the hands. The two women, in modest bonnets and shawls, respond with hesitance and anxiety. Nastasya, sturdy and plain, stands a step below holding a candle to light the way, her face practical and impassive. The atmosphere is anxious and slightly chaotic.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin enthusiastically reassures Raskolnikov’s mother and sister on the landing.

scenes.locationDescription: Street and outside the women's lodgings
scenes.imageDescription: A gloomy evening street in 19th-century St. Petersburg, the cobblestones wet from recent rain. Razumihin strides energetically, supporting Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Avdotya Romanovna, who walk beside him, struggling to keep up. The two women wear long skirts and shawls, Razumihin holds each by the arm, animated and larger-than-life; his boisterous enthusiasm contrasts with the women's weary reserve. Street lamps cast elongated shadows; the mood is uneasy yet hopeful.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin hurries alongside Pulcheria and Dunya, rambling cheerfully in the street.

scenes.locationDescription: Corridor and stairway leading to the women's lodgings
scenes.imageDescription: A dim corridor in a shabby boarding house, lit by a fading candle. Razumihin, flushed and apologetic, stands at the head of the stairs with Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Avdotya Romanovna. He gestures animatedly, while the women, clutching their belongings, listen with a mix of gratitude and embarrassment. The passageway is narrow, with peeling paint and worn floorboards, evoking the poverty and unease of their situation.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin reassures the women as he escorts them to their lodgings upstairs.

scenes.locationDescription: Inside Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Avdotya Romanovna's room
scenes.imageDescription: A humble, simply furnished lodging room with two single beds and a small window letting in the faint glow of street lamps. Pulcheria Alexandrovna sits wearily on one of the beds, handkerchief in hand, her face marked by worry and tears. Avdotya Romanovna, tall and composed yet pale and troubled, paces the floor with folded arms in contemplation. The atmosphere is filled with maternal concern and gentle resignation; period Russian dresses and shawls contribute to the somber mood.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Dounia paces in thought as her anxious mother waits for Razumihin’s return.

scenes.locationDescription: Outside the women's lodgings (corridor)
scenes.imageDescription: In the corridor outside the women's modest boarding room, gaslamps provide scant light. Razumihin, slightly disheveled from the night air and alcohol, stands at the threshold, speaking hurriedly without stepping in. Pulcheria Alexandrovna peers anxiously from the doorway, while Avdotya Romanovna stands behind her, concerned. The hall’s worn walls and creaking floorboards create an atmosphere of fatigue and expectation.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin reassures the family about Raskolnikov's condition before hurrying off.

scenes.locationDescription: Inside Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Avdotya Romanovna's room (with Zossimov's visit)
scenes.imageDescription: The same somber lodging room, now brighter from the glow of a kerosene lamp on the table. Zossimov, a young man with a medical bag and serious manner, stands facing the two women. Pulcheria Alexandrovna sits anxiously, listening intently; Avdotya Romanovna, upright and composed, looks on with attentive curiosity. Zossimov conducts himself with professional reserve. The mood is cautiously optimistic, the setting clean but stark, period attire evident.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Zossimov reassures Raskolnikov’s mother and sister about his condition.

scenes.locationDescription: Street outside the lodgings as Razumihin and Zossimov depart
scenes.imageDescription: A quiet nighttime street in St. Petersburg with lamplight reflecting on the moist cobbles. Razumihin and Zossimov, both in overcoats and hats, converse as they walk away from the boarding house entry. Razumihin is animated and confrontational, grabbing Zossimov by the collar, while Zossimov, startled, tries to shake him off. The night is chilly, the street nearly deserted, period Russian clothing evident. The encounter is tense but laced with dark humor.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin confronts Zossimov on the street after a careless remark.

scenes.locationDescription: Outside Raskolnikov's lodgings, at the landlady's door
scenes.imageDescription: Razumihin and Zossimov stand before a heavy wooden door in a poorly lit stairwell, close to Raskolnikov's lodgings. Razumihin, somber and intense, explains something to Zossimov, who listens with an amused expression. Both wear long coats, and a faint light leaks from under the door. The stairwell’s walls are cracked, and the air feels damp and quiet, full of late-night tension and camaraderie.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin teases Zossimov outside Raskolnikov’s door about his landlady.

entities.excerpt: Razumihin waked up next morning at eight o’clock, troubled and serious. He found himself confronted with many new and unlooked-for perplexities. He had never expected th

…wouldn’t care! He’d be worse!”

He was engaged in such monologues when Zossimov, who had spent the night in Praskovya Pavlovna’s parlour, came in.

He was going home and was in a hurry to look at the invalid first. Razumi…

…me and was in a hurry to look at the invalid first. Razumihin informed him that Raskolnikov was sleeping like a dormouse. Zossimov gave orders that they shouldn’t wake him and promised to see him again about eleven.

…eprived of the honour of an interview with you to-morrow morning by business in the Senate that does not admit of delay, and also that I may not intrude on your family circle while you are meeting your son, and Avdotya Romanovna her brothe…

…a Romanovna... that was simply intolerable. He brought his fist down heavily on the kitchen stove, hurt his hand and sent one of the bricks flying.

“Of course,” he muttered to himself a minute later with a feeling of self-abaseme…

scenes.locationDescription: Razumihin's room early in the morning
scenes.imageDescription: A small, plainly furnished room typical of a St. Petersburg student’s dwelling in the 1860s, cluttered yet reasonably tidy. Early morning light filters through the grimy window onto Razumihin who sits pensively on his bed, partially dressed, with a look of deep reflection. The atmosphere is introspective and tense, hinting at emotional turmoil. Period-appropriate men’s shirt and trousers are visible, and the stove, battered furniture, and books complete the impoverished setting.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin wakes at dawn, wrestling with self-doubt and yesterday's regrets.

scenes.locationDescription: Razumihin's room, during Zossimov's visit
scenes.imageDescription: The same modest, sunlit room. Zossimov, a well-dressed young doctor in a frock coat, stands near the door in conversation with an unshaven Razumihin, who looks abashed, still in the process of getting ready for the day. Both men’s postures reflect the seriousness of their exchange. Physician’s bag in hand, Zossimov glances toward the inner door, signifying concern for the patient in the next room. The overall atmosphere is tense and troubled.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin and Zossimov quietly discuss Raskolnikov’s health at dawn.

scenes.locationDescription: Bakaleyev’s lodgings, Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Dounia’s room
scenes.imageDescription: A cramped but carefully kept boarding room, furnished with simple beds, a table set for breakfast, and personal belongings indicating genteel poverty. Morning light enters through a worn curtain. Pulcheria Alexandrovna, a dignified older woman, warmly greets Razumihin at the door while Avdotya Romanovna (Dounia), poised and serious in a threadbare but neat dress with a delicate scarf, watches. Both women’s clothing shows signs of wear, and there’s an air of restrained anticipation and respect. Razumihin, awkward and self-conscious, stands just inside the room, hat in hand.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin arrives nervously as Pulcheria and Dounia greet him warmly.

scenes.locationDescription: Bakaleyev’s lodgings, at the tea table
scenes.imageDescription: Around the breakfast table in the humble lodgings, the three sit closely together. Tea is being poured, with mismatched crockery and a meagre spread in front of them, their expressions ranging from intent to anxious. Dounia sits upright with folded arms, walking occasionally, while Pulcheria Alexandrovna listens closely. Razumihin, more confident now, leans in as he talks. Soft natural light reveals the threadbare state of the room and the quiet dignity with which the women bear their poverty.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin describes Raskolnikov’s recent life over tea with his mother and sister.

scenes.locationDescription: Bakaleyev’s lodgings, reading Luzhin’s letter
scenes.imageDescription: The breakfast scene continues, but now with a heightened mood. Razumihin unfolds a letter and reads aloud. Both women lean in with anxious attention—Pulcheria Alexandrovna looks distressed, handkerchief in hand, while Dounia controls her features with visible effort. Period-appropriate dresses, a cluttered tea table, and morning light maintain the understated elegance and tension in the setting.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin reads Luzhin’s letter aloud to Raskolnikov’s family.

scenes.locationDescription: The ladies' room, preparing to leave for Raskolnikov’s
scenes.imageDescription: Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Dounia hurriedly prepare to leave, donning their worn hats and mantles. Dounia adjusts her gloves, embarrassed by their condition. Razumihin stands aside respectfully, ready to escort them. The cramped room shows signs of departure—personal effects hurriedly gathered, a travelling bag on the bed. Early daylight, nervous anticipation, and dignity pervade the tableau.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Pulcheria and Dounia anxiously prepare to visit Raskolnikov with Razumihin.

scenes.locationDescription: Street outside Bakaleyev’s house and on the way to Raskolnikov’s
scenes.imageDescription: A gray, bustling St. Petersburg street in the morning, lined with worn stone buildings and mud-slicked cobblestones. Pulcheria Alexandrovna, guided by Dounia and Razumihin, walks anxiously. All are dressed for the weather in modest, dark outerwear. Razumihin, slightly ahead, leads the way with protective energy, while Dounia attends to her mother. The city’s vastness and the trio’s vulnerability are suggested by the towering facades and the somber sky above.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Pulcheria and Dounia worry as they arrive at Raskolnikov's cramped lodgings.

scenes.locationDescription: Entrance and stairway to Raskolnikov’s building
scenes.imageDescription: A dim interior stairwell in a dilapidated St. Petersburg tenement, its wooden steps worn, walls dirty and narrow. Razumihin ascends ahead while Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Dounia follow with trepidation, clutching at their mantles. On the fourth storey, a cracked door opens slightly to reveal two sharp black eyes—the landlady—peering watchfully. Shadows fill the stair, creating an atmosphere of poverty, unease, and expectation.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Pulcheria and Dounia arrive as the landlady spies on them nervously.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's room
scenes.imageDescription: A small, dimly lit St. Petersburg lodging room with faded wallpaper and sparse furniture. Five people fill the space: Raskolnikov, pale and tense, sits upright in a corner chair, dressed in recently washed but plainly worn clothes. Zossimov, smartly attired in a young doctor's black coat, sits on the sofa. Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Dounia, both in modest, slightly travel-worn dresses and shawls, stand close together, concern in their posture as they gaze at Raskolnikov. Razumihin, animated and robust, is seated nearby, while Nastasya in a plain, working woman's dress, stands near the door, observing quietly. Morning daylight enters from a small window, creating a somber, uneasy atmosphere.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov greets his mother and sister as Zossimov assesses his health.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's room
scenes.imageDescription: A modest St. Petersburg boarding house room in the late afternoon, with pale, spare light slanting in over shabby furniture: a narrow iron bed doubling as a sofa, three mismatched chairs. Raskolnikov stands near the center, thin and pale, in rumpled clothes. Sonia, nervously clutching a parasol and wearing a plain old-fashioned dress and hat, stands near the door, shrinking back. Pulcheria Alexandrovna Raskolnikov and Avdotya Romanovna sit together, watching her with curiosity and tension; Razumihin stands politely aside, and there is a sense of awkwardness and social discomfort hanging in the air.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonya enters Raskolnikov's crowded room, uncertain and shy.

scenes.locationDescription: Streets of St. Petersburg
scenes.imageDescription: A bustling 19th-century St. Petersburg street in full daylight. Pulcheria Alexandrovna and Dounia walk closely together among passing crowds pulling carts and pushing furniture. They wear dark, modest dresses and bonnets suited to the period. The city’s architecture looms behind them, with tall, slightly grimy buildings typical of the era. The mood is fretful and anxious, with Pulcheria gesturing and Dounia responding, half lost in the sea of urban life.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia timidly enters the crowded room, surprising Raskolnikov and the others.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's room
scenes.imageDescription: The same sparse room, but now with Raskolnikov and Razumihin standing together near a small, grimy window, talking in low voices. Sonia, standing to the side clutching her parasol and nervously listening, is preparing herself to leave. Shadows stretch across the threadbare carpet, and the room's peeling walls and meager furnishings are stark in the subdued light. The mood is tense and hurried.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia enters Raskolnikov’s room, shy and overwhelmed by the gathering.

scenes.locationDescription: The gateway and street outside Raskolnikov's building
scenes.imageDescription: At the entrance of the old St. Petersburg tenement, near dusk. Raskolnikov, Razumihin, and Sonia pause just outside the stone entryway. Sonia is eager to leave, her eyes cast down as she prepares to turn right, while both men watch her; Raskolnikov with a conflicted expression, Razumihin with open curiosity. The gritty street extends beyond, with the shadows growing longer, and the city’s bustle just out of reach. The mood is uneasy, with a sense that something might be lurking in the background.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia and Raskolnikov speak quietly outside his building before parting ways.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's walk to her lodging and encounter with the unknown gentleman
scenes.imageDescription: A 19th-century city street near a canal, shadowed by evening. Sonia, still in her modest dress and hat, walks swiftly, head down, along the deserted pavement. The unknown gentleman, tall and fashionably dressed, follows her at a discreet distance, his gaze intent. The buildings are tall and slightly dilapidated, the canal glinting dully. Tension and unease color the air as Sonia hurries toward her lodging.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia is quietly followed through the streets by a mysterious gentleman.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia and the unknown gentleman at Kapernaumov's and Madame Resslich's
scenes.imageDescription: A dingy courtyard off the canal, Sonia turning in at the battered wooden gate of a three-story house. The unknown gentleman follows, pausing to check the address and note Sonia’s movements. The names 'Kapernaumov, Tailor' and 'Madame Resslich' are chalked on different doors. Sonia, nervous and self-conscious, slips into her door as the man lingers, cane in hand, in glare of the setting sun. The space is cramped, the buildings leaning close, and the air is heavy with suspicion.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia and a mysterious man arrive as neighbors at their lodgings.

scenes.locationDescription: On the street, walking to Porfiry Petrovitch's flat
scenes.imageDescription: A narrow cobbled street in St. Petersburg under a cloudy sky. Raskolnikov and Razumihin walk side by side, both in drab student coats and hats; Razumihin animatedly gestures, while Raskolnikov listens silently, pale and anxious. The street’s buildings are tall and stern, casting deep shadows. Passersby hurry on their way, giving the men little notice. There is a feeling of foreboding as they near a nondescript grey apartment block.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumihin walk together, discussing the visit to Porfiry.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's flat entrance
scenes.imageDescription: A plain, slightly shabby entryway at the door of a middle-class St. Petersburg flat. Raskolnikov and Razumihin, caught mid-laughter, step in from the dim corridor. The air is heavy, and the faint yellow lamp glow spills from under the door ahead. Razumihin, slightly blushing, seizes Raskolnikov's shoulder to hush him as they cross the threshold.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumihin enter Porfiry’s flat, laughing loudly together.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's apartment, main room
scenes.imageDescription: A spacious, somewhat cluttered government official's apartment in 19th-century St. Petersburg. Early afternoon light filters softly through modest curtains. Raskolnikov, thin and pale in threadbare student attire, stands just inside the door, suppressing laughter. Behind him, Razumihin trails, tall and awkward, flushed red, with a fierce and embarrassed expression. Porfiry Petrovitch, small and rotund in a dressing-gown and slippers, stands in the room's center, assessing them with a curious eye. Zametov observes quietly, seated in a corner, surprised. The air is tense yet tinged with odd amusement, and teacups and a broken glass scatter a nearby small table.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumihin arrive at Porfiry's, disrupting the room with laughter.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's apartment, the sitting area around the sofa
scenes.imageDescription: A grouping of 19th-century upholstered furniture under dim gold light as afternoon wanes. Raskolnikov sits tensely on the edge of a faded red sofa, Porfiry Petrovitch seated across from him with legal papers at hand, his posture attentive and slightly severe. Razumihin, uneasy but supportive, sits at a table opposite, leaning forward in concern, while Zametov stands nearby, arms crossed and skeptical. The room is cluttered with books and legal documents, creating a stuffy and somewhat oppressive atmosphere. The tension between the characters is palpable.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov explains his business to Porfiry as Razumihin listens intently.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's apartment, while Porfiry fetches tea
scenes.imageDescription: With Porfiry Petrovitch absent, the three young men remain amidst a slightly dimmer late afternoon ambience. Raskolnikov, agitated and pale, sits upright, lost in feverish thought, while Razumihin, robust and anxious, glances at him with visible concern. Zametov stands aloof, observing with a calculating gaze from a shadowed corner. The disarrayed furniture and spilled tea-glass remain, creating an uneasy silence that fills the room.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov sits anxiously as Porfiry steps out to order tea.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's apartment, resuming conversation around the table
scenes.imageDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch returns, lively and smiling, to rejoin Raskolnikov and Razumihin, now joined again by Zametov, around a well-used table cluttered with notebooks and samovar. Outside, the daylight fades, casting a gentle glow on the gathering faces. Porfiry, in his robe, gestures animatedly as Razumihin leans in to argue. Raskolnikov sits slightly apart, tense and contemplative, his face increasingly pale. Zametov stays in the background, amused and watchful. The scene is charged with intellectual debate and underlying suspicion.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Porfiry sparks a lively debate on crime and society with Razumihin and Raskolnikov.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's apartment, the intense conclusion and departure
scenes.imageDescription: In the late afternoon hush, all four men stand near the apartment's exit. Raskolnikov, cap in hand, wears a grave and haunted expression. Porfiry approaches with excessive politeness, a faint smile covering calculating eyes. Razumihin scowls, frowning in confusion and irritation, while Zametov stands aside, arms folded, his posture skeptical. The tense, heavily-furnished hallway and muted wallpaper contribute to a stifling, oppressive mood as farewells are exchanged and suspicions linger.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Porfiry pointedly questions Raskolnikov about extraordinary men before his departure.

scenes.locationDescription: The street outside Porfiry Petrovitch's apartment
scenes.imageDescription: The gray, chilly street of St. Petersburg near an official residence. Gloomy skies cast a cold, diffused light on the damp cobblestones. Raskolnikov and Razumihin walk side by side, both tense and weary. Raskolnikov draws a deep breath, his pallor stark against the somber city backdrop, while Razumihin remains silent, face clouded with concern. The street is largely empty, silent but for the faint echo of their footsteps.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumikhin leave Porfiry’s apartment in tense silence.

scenes.locationDescription: Outside Bakaleyev’s lodgings, on the street
scenes.imageDescription: Two young men in mid-19th-century Russian dress stand in heated discussion on a bustling St. Petersburg street in early summer, the air thick and hot. The architecture is that of stone apartment buildings with ornate facades. Raskolnikov is pale, thin, and somber in a threadbare suit; Razumihin, more robust and energetic, gestures passionately. The light is sharp, sunlight reflecting off cobblestones, and passers-by go about their business.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumihin debate suspicions as they near Bakaleyev’s lodgings.

scenes.locationDescription: Steps outside Bakaleyev’s lodgings
scenes.imageDescription: At the worn stone entrance steps of an apartment building, Raskolnikov turns away with agitation while Razumihin stands on the step, looking after him in frustration and concern. The setting sun casts long shadows; the air is tense. Both are in modest, slightly shabby mid-19th-century Russian clothing.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov abruptly leaves Razumihin on the steps outside Bakaleyev’s lodgings.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov’s room
scenes.imageDescription: A small, sparse garret room with peeling wallpaper and a rickety bed, bathed in harsh afternoon light streaming through a grimy window. Raskolnikov, sweat-soaked and anxious, kneels by a wall, urgently checking beneath loose wallpaper at a low corner. The room feels cramped, oppressive, and tense; outside distant street noise is muffled.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov anxiously checks his hiding place before leaving his room in a daze.

scenes.locationDescription: Threshold near the porter’s quarters and on the street outside Raskolnikov’s building
scenes.imageDescription: Near the entrance of a communal apartment, a stout porter in a simple uniform gestures toward Raskolnikov, who approaches wearily. Nearby, a short, stooped man in a long coat and greasy cap watches from a distance. The narrow, cobbled street outside is illuminated by late afternoon light. The scene is tense and mysterious.
scenes.imageSubtitle: A mysterious stranger quietly inquires about Raskolnikov at his building.

scenes.locationDescription: Along the street and then at a crossroads
scenes.imageDescription: A dusky St. Petersburg street, evening falling, with Raskolnikov following the stooped, long-coated man at a measured distance. The crossroads are empty, buildings rising around, and moonlight begins to edge the scene with silver. The tension between the two is palpable, their figures isolated in the growing gloom.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov is confronted by a mysterious stranger on a busy street.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov’s garret room
scenes.imageDescription: In the small, sparsely furnished attic room, Raskolnikov collapses onto a narrow sofa, exhausted and pale. The light is fading, dusk pooling in corners and edging his haggard features. The atmosphere is oppressive, with an almost physical weight of anxiety and isolation.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov collapses in exhaustion, lost in restless, fragmented memories.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov’s garret room — Razumihin and Nastasya’s visit (brief)
scenes.imageDescription: Razumihin, tall and earnest, stands just inside the doorway of Raskolnikov’s small room, accompanied by Nastasya, a plain servant woman in simple attire. Raskolnikov lies motionless on the sofa, feigning sleep. Early evening light casts dim shadows; both visitors converse quietly, a mood of concern and quiet respect pervades.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin and Nastasya quietly check on a resting Raskolnikov.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov’s garret room — Raskolnikov’s delirium, then dream sequence
scenes.imageDescription: Raskolnikov sits alone on his narrow bed, his face contorted by feverish thoughts. The room blurs at the edges, moonlight faintly illuminating his tormented figure. He appears rapt in hallucination, sickly sweat on his brow, his eyes wild, with shadowy images—faces, childhood scenes—suggested indistinctly in the gloom and confusion of his dreaming mind.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov grapples with feverish doubts and haunting dreams in his garret.

scenes.locationDescription: Dream — Pawnbroker’s flat and the staircase
scenes.imageDescription: A surreal, moon-flooded interior of an old St. Petersburg apartment. Raskolnikov stands tense in the moonlit parlor, with the bent figure of the old pawnbroker, Alyona Ivanovna, her face unseen, sitting and shaking with noiseless laughter in the corner. A shadowy open doorway hints at unseen figures laughing and whispering beyond. The atmosphere is dreamlike, oppressive with dread, and spectral faces seem to crowd the stairs outside in sinister silence.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov follows a mysterious figure up a moonlit staircase in a strange dream.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov’s garret room — awakening and Svidrigaïlov’s entrance
scenes.imageDescription: The cramped garret now lit by the pale light of dusk. Raskolnikov, gaunt and disheveled, sits on his bed in confusion. In the open doorway stands Svidrigaïlov, a stout man with a fair, almost white beard, in fashionable attire, holding a hat and cane. The mood bristles with silent tension; Svidrigaïlov’s posture is patient, intent, and watchful.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Svidrigaïlov quietly enters Raskolnikov's room as he pretends to sleep.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's room
scenes.imageDescription: A cramped St. Petersburg garret bathed in the cool gray daylight from a single window. Two men face off across a battered table: Raskolnikov, bedraggled and pale, sits slumped on a narrow bed, while Mr. Svidrigaïlov, robust and well-dressed in a fashionable frock coat and top hat, stands near the door with an enigmatic smile. The atmosphere is tense and close, with peeling wallpaper and sparse 1860s Russian furnishings emphasizing the poverty of the setting.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov is startled by an unexpected visit from Svidrigaïlov.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's doorway
scenes.imageDescription: In the immediately outside hall of a crowded tenement, the heavy wooden door to Raskolnikov's room swings open as Mr. Svidrigaïlov, still in elegant dress, nearly collides with Razumihin, a strong, broad-shouldered young man in a rumpled student coat. The dim corridor is lined with worn floorboards and light from a high window casts elongated shadows, capturing the surprise on Razumihin's face and the composed demeanor of Svidrigaïlov.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin and Svidrigaïlov cross paths in Raskolnikov's doorway.

scenes.locationDescription: In the street, hurrying toward Bakaleyev’s
scenes.imageDescription: Two young men, dressed in threadbare mid-19th century Russian coats and hats, walk briskly side by side along a dimly lit, bustling St. Petersburg street at dusk. Street lamps cast a faint yellow glow on damp cobblestones. Raskolnikov looks troubled and gaunt, Razumihin attentive and concerned, each huddled against the cold. The city looms around them, with pedestrians and horse-drawn carts in the background.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov confides his fears to Razumihin as they hurry through the street.

scenes.locationDescription: In the corridor outside Bakaleyev’s apartment
scenes.imageDescription: A narrow, shadowy corridor in a St. Petersburg apartment building. Raskolnikov and Razumihin, wearing overcoats, stand at one end as Pyotr Petrovitch Luzhin, formally dressed in a dark suit with an overcoat over his arm, searches for a door number. The hallway is lit by sparse candlelight, creating an air of anticipation and unease.
scenes.imageSubtitle: The family gathers around the samovar as Luzhin arrives for the meeting.

scenes.locationDescription: In the family’s rented room at Bakaleyev’s
scenes.imageDescription: A cramped but tidy St. Petersburg sitting room lit by the warm glow of an oil lamp. A samovar steams on a crowded round table at center. Pulcheria Alexandrovna, dignified in modest dark clothing, greets Luzhin at the door; Dounia offers a gentle embrace to Raskolnikov. Razumihin stands close by, while Luzhin enters with stiff formality. The furnishings are simple, reflecting genteel poverty. The atmosphere is tense, with undercurrents of awkwardness.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumihin discuss Svidrigaïlov and their concerns for Dounia.

scenes.locationDescription: Sitting at the table, tea and strained discussion
scenes.imageDescription: The small, dimly lit family room, with all assembled at the round table: Dounia and Luzhin opposite each other, Raskolnikov beside his sister, Pulcheria to one side, Razumihin next to Luzhin. Their faces are tense and wary; Luzhin ostentatiously dabs his nose with a scented handkerchief, while the samovar steams gently. Simple chairs and plain wallpaper complete the modest, slightly worn décor.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Tension rises as Luzhin questions Dounia and her mother over tea.

scenes.locationDescription: Still at the table, family confrontation escalates
scenes.imageDescription: The same modest sitting room and table. Dounia, pale and upset, looks directly at Luzhin, who sits upright and aggrieved, his gloved hand on his hat. Pulcheria Alexandrovna, anxious, keeps her hands clasped. Razumihin leans tensely forward, while Raskolnikov sits withdrawn, fingers on his teacup. The air is charged with emotion, and the diminishing daylight filters in through thin curtains.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov reveals Svidrigaïlov's visit as tensions mount at the family table.

scenes.locationDescription: At the table, Luzhin and the family quarrel openly
scenes.imageDescription: At the same round table, Luzhin sits rigidly, his face growing red with indignation as he argues. Dounia faces him, flushed with anger; Raskolnikov watches coolly, his expression sardonic; Pulcheria Alexandrovna looks deeply unsettled, and Razumihin fidgets nervously. The lamp casts stark shadows, highlighting the family’s distress and alienation from Luzhin.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Dounia and Luzhin argue heatedly as tensions rise at the family table.

scenes.locationDescription: Conclusion of the confrontation; Luzhin leaves
scenes.imageDescription: Luzhin, visibly pale and shaken, stands near the open doorway, clutching his hat, preparing to leave. Dounia, upright and resolute, stands a few feet away, white with anger. Pulcheria Alexandrovna, on her feet, gestures him away, while Razumihin restrains himself from intervening. Raskolnikov stands between Luzhin and the family, expression icy and determined. The air is taut with finality as Luzhin prepares to leave the modest, lamplit room.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Luzhin angrily leaves after Dounia firmly ends their engagement.

scenes.locationDescription: Pyotr Petrovitch Luzhin's lodgings (interior monologue)
scenes.imageDescription: A dimly lit, modestly furnished room typical of mid-19th-century St. Petersburg, with dark wooden furniture and a mirror on the wall. Pyotr Petrovitch Luzhin stands alone near a window, sharply dressed in a well-kept, somber suit reflecting his self-importance. The evening light filters through heavy curtains, casting deep shadows and lending an atmosphere of introspection and injured pride.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Luzhin broods alone, nursing wounded pride and plotting his next move.

scenes.locationDescription: The Raskolnikovs' lodgings, main room
scenes.imageDescription: A close, candle-lit room with plain wallpaper and simple, slightly worn furniture—an oval table at the center surrounded by three women and a man. Avdotya Romanovna (Dounia) embraces her mother, Pulcheria Raskolnikov, both dressed in modest, conservative dresses appropriate to the period. Rodion Raskolnikov sits apart on a chair, pale and withdrawn in his drab student attire. Razumihin stands nearby, animated, in slightly disheveled clothing. The setting is intimate and emotional, with an undercurrent of relief and anxiety.
scenes.imageSubtitle: The family discusses Luzhin's departure and Svidrigaïlov's strange offer.

scenes.locationDescription: The Raskolnikovs' lodgings, main room (later—business discussion)
scenes.imageDescription: The same small living room, illuminated by candles and a flickering fireplace, with the atmosphere now warmer and more hopeful. Razumihin stands or sits forward, energetically explaining his publishing plan, gesturing enthusiastically. Dounia leans in, interested and bright-eyed, her mother looking uncertain but attentive. Raskolnikov is more detached, listening in silence, his expression inscrutable. Their dress and positioning suggest a family conference in the tense optimism of new possibilities.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin eagerly shares his publishing business idea with the group.

scenes.locationDescription: The Raskolnikovs' lodgings, doorway and corridor
scenes.imageDescription: Near the doorway in a cramped, poorly lit Russian tenement corridor, Raskolnikov stands ready to depart, clutching his cap, his face drawn and determined. Dounia and Pulcheria Alexandrovna approach him anxiously, their faces etched with fear and confusion. Razumihin hovers by the doorway, tense and uncertain, while candlelight throws long shadows and the night presses in outside. The mood is one of sorrow and strained parting.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov bids a troubled farewell to his mother and sister in the hallway.

scenes.locationDescription: Corridor, outside the Raskolnikovs' rooms
scenes.imageDescription: A narrow corridor lit by a single flickering oil lamp, its walls faded and damp from years of use. Raskolnikov, thin and intense in his threadbare coat, stands facing Razumihin, whose shocked pallor is evident as they confront each other. Sparse decor, worn wooden floorboards, and deep shadows all evoke the poverty and tension of the moment. The oppressive St. Petersburg gloom seeps in, underscoring the gravity and finality of their exchange.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Razumihin exchange tense words in the dim corridor.

scenes.locationDescription: Back in the Raskolnikovs' lodgings
scenes.imageDescription: The Raskolnikovs' parlor, softly lit by candlelight, feels quieter and heavier. Razumihin returns to the two women, attempting to comfort Pulcheria Alexandrovna, who sits weeping, her hands folded. Dounia stands behind her, distraught but resolute. The furnishings show a humble life, with a shawl draped over a chair and tea things abandoned on the table. The sense is of both distress and a dawning solidarity, with Razumihin now claiming a new closeness to the family.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Razumihin promises to care for Raskolnikov and reassures his family.

scenes.locationDescription: Outside Sonia's apartment house on the canal bank
scenes.imageDescription: A narrow, foggy street by a St. Petersburg canal at nightfall. An old, three-storey green building looms, its windows dimly lit. Raskolnikov, gaunt and tense, stands at the entrance, speaking with a weary porter in a drab greatcoat. The setting is bleak, shadows lengthening across cracked flagstones. 19th-century Russian urban poverty is evident in the building’s faded paint and broken fences. Mist rises off the canal, adding a somber, oppressive atmosphere.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov searches for Sonia's apartment in the dark stairwell.

scenes.locationDescription: On the staircase and gallery outside Sonia's door
scenes.imageDescription: Inside a dim, claustrophobic tenement corridor on the second floor. Raskolnikov, his face half-lit by the faint glow from a crack under a door, hesitates in a shadowy wooden gallery. A worn wooden floor contrasts with the sooty, peeling walls. The area feels cold, with shaky railings visible through an open window overlooking a dank courtyard below. The only light comes from a door that has just begun to open, casting a pale beam onto Raskolnikov’s cautious silhouette.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov searches for Sonia’s room in the dimly lit gallery.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's room in the Kapernaumovs' apartment
scenes.imageDescription: A poor, irregularly-shaped room with slanted walls and three small windows looking onto the canal, their panes fogged by night. Sonia stands anxiously near the door holding a candle in a battered copper candlestick, lighting the sparse, shabby furnishings: a plain bedstead, threadbare blue cloth on a battered table, a lonely chest of drawers. Raskolnikov, tense and pale, sits at the table, his coat draped open. Sonia’s face is flushed and agitated. The yellow wallpaper is dirty and peeling, and all is bathed in flickering candlelight, emphasizing the somber, oppressive poverty of the setting.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov visits Sonia in her modest, dimly lit room.

scenes.locationDescription: Still in Sonia's room, during Raskolnikov and Sonia's intense discussion
scenes.imageDescription: The same cramped room, candle flickering lower. Raskolnikov sits at the table, hands restless, face shadowed and intense. Sonia stands before him, pale, wringing her hands, her eyes glossy with unshed tears. The corners seem even darker, as if the meager light cannot reach them. The atmosphere is charged with anxiety and emotional tension, the room’s emptiness a reflection of the characters’ distress.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov questions Sonia about her room as tension fills the air.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's room, Raskolnikov's anguished outburst and Sonia's distress
scenes.imageDescription: Raskolnikov paces agitatedly in the cramped, dim room, while Sonia stands near the table, shoulders hunched, overwhelmed with sorrow. Her face is downcast, knuckles pressed to her lips. Only the candle’s weak light gives shape to the battered furniture and the threadbare floor. The emotional strain crackles between them, made sharper by the poverty and close quarters. The peeling wallpaper and discarded items show a life of hardship.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov lashes out as Sonia is overcome with grief in her small room.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's room, discussion of God, reading the Gospel
scenes.imageDescription: By the dying candlelight, Raskolnikov stands near Sonia as she clutches a worn leather-bound Russian New Testament at the cluttered table. Her eyes shine with emotion; Raskolnikov looks at her searchingly, questioning. The air is heavy with tension and spiritual conflict. The night deepens outside the windows, reflected in the gloom within. Both appear isolated by their suffering, the meager candlelight highlighting their strained faces and hands.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia and Raskolnikov discuss faith as she clutches her worn New Testament.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's room, the reading of the Raising of Lazarus
scenes.imageDescription: Sonia sits at the table, trembling, reading aloud from the Bible, the candle nearly spent. Raskolnikov sits opposite, unmoving, elbows on the table, staring away into shadow. The battered candlestick casts a fragile glow across their pale faces and the threadbare blue tablecloth. The sparse furniture and walls loom in the low light, intensifying the spiritual gravity of the moment, as if the poverty-stricken space itself listens to the ancient words.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia struggles to read the story of Lazarus aloud to Raskolnikov.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's room, Raskolnikov reveals his desperation and hints of his guilt
scenes.imageDescription: Raskolnikov, wild-eyed and feverish, faces Sonia in the dim, nearly candleless room. She stands near the wall, her hands clasped in anxiety, recoiling in fear and confusion. His posture is rigid, his expression stern and haunted. The dying candle sends long, uneven shadows across their figures and the empty bed, amplifying the sense of finality and desperation that fills the room.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov desperately confides in Sonia, seeking her understanding and support.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's room after Raskolnikov's departure, Sonia alone
scenes.imageDescription: Sonia stands alone in the gloom of her meager room. The last candle stub flares weakly, casting a flickering glow over her anguished, bewildered face as she stares at the closed door. The room is cast in deep shadow, highlighting the oppressive poverty and the emotional storm within her. Her hands are raised anxiously to her head, the chest of drawers and narrow bed stand forlorn beneath the slanting walls, all suffused with an atmosphere of dread and loneliness.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia sits alone, troubled and feverish after Raskolnikov’s strange visit.

scenes.locationDescription: Sonia's room and the empty flat next door, Svidrigaïlov eavesdropping
scenes.imageDescription: The perspective shifts to the unlit, shabby empty room adjoining Sonia’s. In the darkness, Mr. Svidrigaïlov stands by the door with his ear pressed to the wood, listening intently to the muffled sounds from Sonia’s side. A thin beam of candlelight seeps under the connecting door. Svidrigaïlov’s face, sharp-featured and alert, is partly illuminated from the gloom. The setting exudes clandestine tension, secrecy, and voyeurism, with period-appropriate overcoat and cravat on Svidrigaïlov.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Svidrigaïlov quietly listens in on Sonia and Raskolnikov's conversation next door.

scenes.locationDescription: Police department waiting room
scenes.imageDescription: A cramped, bureaucratic waiting room inside a St. Petersburg police department, morning light faintly illuminating the dusty windows. Several government clerks in plain, mid-19th-century attire sit at desks writing, others walk purposefully to and fro. Raskolnikov, thin and haggard, stands anxiously near the door, his posture tense, dressed in a worn coat and cap. The room feels indifferent, busy, with no one paying special attention to him.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov waits anxiously in the police department, watched by indifferent clerks.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's office
scenes.imageDescription: A government-furnished study, modest and functional, bathed in cool daylight from a single window. Porfiry Petrovitch, round and jovial with an underlying sharpness, stands by his writing table, facing Raskolnikov, who has just entered and stands uncertainly near the doorway. The walls are lined with yellow-polished wooden furniture: a sofa, bookcase, and several chairs. The atmosphere is taut with psychological tension despite the official, almost domestic orderliness.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov visits Porfiry Petrovitch for a tense conversation in his study.

scenes.locationDescription: Porfiry Petrovitch's office
scenes.imageDescription: Inside a cramped, shadowy 19th-century St. Petersburg police office, with wooden floors and austere furnishing. Porfiry Petrovitch, a balding, sharp-faced man in an official's frock coat, stands near the door in agitation. A group of officials and warders, dressed in dark uniforms, crowd the threshold as Nikolay enters, pallid and nervous, in simple workman's attire. Raskolnikov, thin and intense, stands off to one side, half concealed in a corner, eyes wide. Tense, anxious atmosphere; gaslight flickers against the dusk outside.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Nikolay bursts in and confesses as Porfiry and others look on in shock.

scenes.locationDescription: Corridor outside Porfiry's office and staircase
scenes.imageDescription: A dingy police office corridor with worn wooden flooring, government staff and idle onlookers lining the passage, some in drab uniforms, others in workers’ garb. Raskolnikov is being shown out by Porfiry, who holds his arm. The two porters, large, rough men in peasants’ attire, stand nearby, eyes fixed on Raskolnikov. Gaslight glows dimly against yellowed walls, casting elongated shadows. The sense is of pressurized scrutiny, tension in the close air.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov and Porfiry share a tense exchange on the office staircase.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's room
scenes.imageDescription: A small, shabby St. Petersburg garret with peeling wallpaper and sparse furnishings—a narrow wooden bed, a battered desk, a threadbare rug. Raskolnikov sits hunched on a sofa, face in hands, gaunt and lost in grim contemplation, his coat still on. Daylight filters weakly through a grimy window, accentuating the gloom and isolation of the room, adding an air of bleak tension.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov sits alone, troubled by his encounter with Porfiry.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's room—entrance of the 'man of yesterday'
scenes.imageDescription: Raskolnikov stands near the door of his cramped, dimly lit room, startled and wary. The door opens to reveal the 'man of yesterday/phantom,' a modestly dressed workman with hangdog expression, pausing in the doorway, shadows enveloping his face. Late afternoon light filters in, deepening the somber, uneasy air. Raskolnikov’s thin figure is tense, back slightly arched in surprise.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov is confronted by the remorseful man from the previous night's scene.

scenes.locationDescription: Raskolnikov's room—after the man leaves
scenes.imageDescription: The man of yesterday is departing through the open door, head bowed. Raskolnikov stands in his small, dimly lit room, posture now straighter, a subtle look of grim resolve on his face. The remnants of the humble, threadbare furnishings and the heavy, stagnant air maintain the oppressive, 19th-century urban Russian mood.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov steels himself after the mysterious visitor leaves his room.

“We had a debate lat…

…t afterwards they’d see I’d done them a service. You know, Terebyeva (who is in the community now) was blamed because when she left her family and... devoted... herself, she wrote to her father and mother that she wouldn’t go on living con…

scenes.locationDescription: In Lebeziatnikov and Luzhin's lodging, morning
scenes.imageDescription: A small, sparsely furnished Petersburg lodging in the gray winter morning light, with Pyotr Petrovitch Luzhin standing and peering into a modest looking-glass, clearly troubled and fussing. In the background, Mr. Lebeziatnikov, an anemic young man with pale flaxen mutton-chop whiskers, lounges awkwardly, casting a sarcastic glance at Luzhin. Both wear period-appropriate 19th-century Russian men's indoor clothing—Luzhin more formal, Lebeziatnikov in threadbare, slightly ill-fitting attire. The atmosphere is tense and somber.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Luzhin broods over his failed engagement as Lebeziatnikov watches in silence.

scenes.locationDescription: The hallway outside Katerina Ivanovna’s room
scenes.imageDescription: A dim, narrow hallway in a rundown Petersburg tenement, lined with faded wallpaper and lit by a single thin shaft of daylight from a dusty window. Pyotr Petrovitch Luzhin stands in the corridor, pausing with narrowed eyes as he observes preparations—Amalia Fyodorovna Lippevechsel, a substantial German woman in new black silk, busily sets a table with mismatched crockery. The air is lively and slightly chaotic as other unseen tenants bustle in the background.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Pyotr Petrovitch learns of the upcoming funeral dinner preparations.

scenes.locationDescription: Back in Lebeziatnikov’s room, Luzhin and Lebeziatnikov converse
scenes.imageDescription: The small, slightly untidy room is lit by pale daylight, with Lebeziatnikov, thin and nervous, wandering as Luzhin counts bundles of banknotes at a cluttered table. Papers, books, and a reckoning frame are visible. The wallpaper is threadbare. Both men have tense postures; Luzhin sits absorbed in his money while Lebeziatnikov moves anxiously, gesturing as he speaks.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Luzhin discusses politics and his ambitions with Lebeziatnikov in their shared lodgings.

scenes.locationDescription: Continuing in Lebeziatnikov’s room, philosophical debate on progress
scenes.imageDescription: The same cramped lodging room, now alive with animated gesturing. Lebeziatnikov stands near the table, passionately expounding, while Luzhin sits, tapping a bead frame, his face twisted with irony. Light from the small window glimmers off the notes on the table. Both men’s 19th-century attire is rumpled from argument. The mood is increasingly irritable, with philosophical pamphlets carelessly strewn about.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Lebeziatnikov and Luzhin debate progress amid tensions over a neighbor’s gathering.

scenes.locationDescription: Lebeziatnikov’s room, conversation about Sofya Semyonovna and 'the community'
scenes.imageDescription: Still within the same spare Petersburg lodgings, the two men engage in heated conversation, markedly animated—Lebeziatnikov, flushed and nervous, stands as Luzhin leans back with a sly grin, his posture haughty. Their debate brings an undercurrent of scorn and defensiveness. A patch of cold daylight from the window highlights the dust in the room. The setting remains modest but charged.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Lebeziatnikov defends Sofya and discusses societal roles with Luzhin.

scenes.locationDescription: Lebeziatnikov’s room, arranging to meet Sonia
scenes.imageDescription: Luzhin, having finished counting money, stands beside the table, arranging his notes, seeming distracted and more scheming. Lebeziatnikov, tense and slightly resentful, lingers near the window, arms crossed. The winter light streams in, highlighting their wary expressions. The atmosphere turns more secretive as the room prepares for a new visitor.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Lebeziatnikov reluctantly agrees to summon Sonia at Luzhin's request.

scenes.locationDescription: Lebeziatnikov’s room, Sonia arrives for Luzhin’s meeting
scenes.imageDescription: The small, plain room now contains three figures: Luzhin, sitting at the table with a stack of banknotes and a gold pince-nez; Lebeziatnikov, anxious but attentive, stands by the window. Sonia, young and painfully shy, stands hesitantly at the doorway, wearing a simple, threadbare 19th-century dress and clutching her shawl. The light is wan and nervous tension fills the scene.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia arrives nervously as Luzhin prepares to speak with her and Lebeziatnikov.

scenes.locationDescription: Lebeziatnikov’s room, Luzhin manipulates Sonia
scenes.imageDescription: Sonia, seated at the table, nervously avoids Luzhin’s gaze, blushing and twisting her hands. Luzhin adopts an exaggeratedly dignified pose, spectacles in hand, facing her across the table where some notes lie. Lebeziatnikov stands quietly at the side, keeping a careful watch. The furnishings are sparse, and the air is taut with discomfort and Luzhin’s patronizing attitude.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Luzhin corners Sonia, speaking to her with calculated politeness and hidden motives.

scenes.locationDescription: Lebeziatnikov’s room, Sonia takes leave in distress
scenes.imageDescription: Sonia, red-faced and on the verge of tears, stands hastily up from her chair, clutching a ten-rouble note. Luzhin stands and bows with forced ceremony at the doorway, while Lebeziatnikov looks on solemnly in the background. The emotional strain is evident in Sonia’s posture and expression, as the cold morning light glances off the scattered money and simple furniture.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia hurriedly leaves Lebeziatnikov’s room after receiving money from Luzhin.

scenes.locationDescription: Lebeziatnikov’s room, after Sonia leaves: Luzhin and Lebeziatnikov reflect
scenes.imageDescription: The room now contains just Luzhin and Lebeziatnikov. Luzhin seems preoccupied, rubbing his hands as if calculating, while Lebeziatnikov faces him earnestly, one hand out in approval. The table is a clutter of rubles and papers. Shadows lengthen in the gray light, as the atmosphere mixes awkward respect and underlying distrust.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Lebeziatnikov praises Luzhin's charity as they discuss marriage and social ideas.

scenes.locationDescription: In Katerina Ivanovna's crowded lodging dining room
scenes.imageDescription: A cramped St. Petersburg lodging house dining room is set for a memorial dinner; a mismatched tablecloth, assorted plates and glasses, and simple wooden furniture fill the space. Katerina Ivanovna, thin and agitated, gives hurried instructions; Amalia Ivanovna, stiff in her black silk dress and mourning cap, supervises preparations with affected dignity. The unfortunate little Pole hurries about with provisions. The scene is bustling, with somber, 19th-century Russian working-class attire, subdued daylight filtering through a cloudy window, and a tense, prideful atmosphere.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Katerina Ivanovna hosts a memorial dinner for Marmeladov in their small lodgings.

scenes.locationDescription: In the same room, now with the guests assembled for dinner
scenes.imageDescription: A motley group of poorly dressed guests—clerks, Poles, and the nearly-blind old man—fill the dinner table in the lodging house parlor. Katerina Ivanovna sits at the head with austere dignity; Amalia Ivanovna is on her right, prideful in her mourning attire. The children eat at a distance on a bench in the far corner under Polenka's watchful eye. White daylight exposes the poverty of the setting, while the guests' manners alternate between awkwardness, drunkenness, and affected civility.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Katerina Ivanovna surveys the assembled, disappointed by the absent guests.

scenes.locationDescription: The dinner table as Raskolnikov arrives
scenes.imageDescription: Rodion Raskolnikov, pale and thin, politely greets the assembled company as he enters the cluttered dining room. Katerina Ivanovna brightens at his arrival and seats him on her left, while Amalia Ivanovna sits stiffly opposite him. The poverty and mixture of dress styles are evident; Sonia is not yet present. Oil lamps and daylight mingle, casting shadows over the mismatched crockery and guests arrayed awkwardly around the table. The atmosphere is strained, buzzing with humiliation, pride, and suppressed tension.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Raskolnikov joins Katerina Ivanovna at the lively, chaotic memorial dinner.

scenes.locationDescription: At the dinner table, with Sonia's arrival
scenes.imageDescription: Sonia enters, quietly and hesitantly, wearing a modest dark brown dress. She bows to Raskolnikov as she sits beside him. Katerina Ivanovna, flushed and animated, continues speaking fervently, gesturing with a blood-spotted handkerchief. The guests observe with various states of sobriety and curiosity. The children at the edge of the room watch anxiously. The lighting is predominantly from an overcast window, muted and dreary, emphasizing the melancholy but tense mood that fills this working-class Russian gathering.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Sonia arrives late and takes her seat beside Raskolnikov during the funeral meal.

scenes.locationDescription: At the dinner table as tension escalates and dinner ends
scenes.imageDescription: The scene is animated: Katerina Ivanovna, eyes bright with fever, stands and gestures with a crumpled certificate, launching into dreams of her future school. Sonia sits beside her, blushing as Katerina kisses her on the cheek. The drunken clerks snicker; the Pole looks on with confusion. Amalia Ivanovna grows stiff and resentful, hands folded, lips pursed. Teacups appear as the dinner concludes. The atmosphere thickens with excitement, irritation, and mounting discord, lamps glowing dimly in the gray afternoon.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Katerina Ivanovna proudly shares her plans for a future girls’ school at dinner.

scenes.locationDescription: The same room as the quarrel erupts
scenes.imageDescription: Anger flashes across the dining room as Katerina Ivanovna, standing now, argues fiercely with Amalia Ivanovna, who grows crimson and gesticulates in rage. The guests—some delighted, some nervous—urge the quarrel on, the children start to cry, and Sonia moves to restrain Katerina Ivanovna, worry etched on her face. The mismatched, poorly-lit room is thick with the chaos and noise of a scandalous public confrontation in a dilapidated Russian tenement.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Katerina Ivanovna and Amalia Ivanovna trade heated insults at the dinner table.

scenes.locationDescription: The dining room, as Luzhin enters
scenes.imageDescription: Pyotr Petrovitch Luzhin stands tall and severe in the doorway, surveying the tumultuous scene with a cold, scrutinizing gaze. Katerina Ivanovna, visibly agitated, rushes towards him. The party is frozen, guests mid-gesture; Sonia hovers near Katerina, and Amalia Ivanovna, red-faced, clutches at the silver spoons. The lighting is stark as day fades; the atmosphere is tense, shocked, and anticipatory, capturing a critical moment of interruption in the shabby, crowded Russian parlor.
scenes.imageSubtitle: Luzhin arrives at the gathering as Katerina Ivanovna hurries to greet him.
